2604.06267.title: MO-RiskVAE: A Multi-Omics Variational Autoencoder for Survival Risk Modeling in Multiple MyelomaMO-RiskVAE
2604.06267.paper_summary: Multimodal variational autoencoders (VAEs) have emerged as a powerful framework for survival risk modeling in multiple myeloma by integrating heterogeneous omics and clinical data. However, when trained under survival supervision, standard latent regularization strategies often fail to preserve prognostically relevant variation, leading to unstable or overly constrained representations. Despite numerous proposed variants, it remains unclear which aspects of latent design fundamentally govern performance in this setting. In this work, we conduct a controlled investigation of latent modeling choices for multimodal survival prediction within a unified extension of the MyeVAE framework. By systematically isolating regularization scale, posterior geometry, and latent space structure under identical architectures and optimization protocols, we show that survival-driven training is primarily sensitive to the magnitude and structure of latent regularization rather than the specific divergence formulation. In particular, moderate relaxation of KL regularization consistently improves survival discrimination, while alternative divergence mechanisms such as MMD and HSIC provide limited benefit without appropriate scaling. We further demonstrate that structuring the latent space can improve alignment between learned representations and survival risk gradients. A hybrid continuous--discrete formulation based on Gumbel--Softmax enhances global risk ordering in the continuous latent subspace, even though stable discrete subtype discovery does not emerge under survival supervision. Guided by these findings, we instantiate a robust multimodal survival model, termed MO-RiskVAE, which consistently improves risk stratification over the original MyeVAE without introducing additional supervision or complex training heuristics.
2604.06267.paper_summary_zh: 多模態變分自編碼器（VAEs）已成為多發性骨髓瘤中生存風險建模的強大框架，通過整合異質的組學和臨床數據。然而，在生存監督下訓練時，標準的潛在正則化策略往往無法保留預後相關的變異，導致不穩定或過度約束的表示。儘管提出了許多變種，但在這種情況下，潛在設計的哪些方面根本上影響性能仍不清楚。在本研究中，我們對多模態生存預測中的潛在建模選擇進行了受控調查，並在MyeVAE框架的統一擴展下進行。通過在相同的架構和優化協議下系統地隔離正則化尺度、後驗幾何和潛在空間結構，我們表明生存驅動的訓練主要對潛在正則化的大小和結構敏感，而不是特定的散度公式。特別是，適度放鬆KL正則化始終能改善生存區分，而其他散度機制如MMD和HSIC在沒有適當縮放的情況下提供的好處有限。我們進一步展示了結構化潛在空間可以改善學習表示與生存風險梯度之間的對齊。基於Gumbel--Softmax的混合連續--離散公式在連續潛在子空間中增強了全局風險排序，即使在生存監督下穩定的離散亞型發現並未出現。根據這些發現，我們實現了一個穩健的多模態生存模型，稱為MO-RiskVAE，該模型在不引入額外監督或複雜訓練啟發式的情況下，始終改善了相對於原始MyeVAE的風險分層。
2604.06267.author: Zixuan Chen et.al.
2604.06267.authors: Zixuan Chen, Heng Zhang, YuPeng Qin, WenPeng Xing, Qiang Wang, Da Wang, Changting Lin, Meng Han
